question_title: What will the ICE Brent crude oil front-month futures settlement price be on June 12, 2026?
summary:
ICE Brent crude futures (CBQ26) settlement price forecast for June 12, 2026. Starting from the current CBQ26 price of ~$97 (June 3-4 settlement), I model 5 scenarios over 6 trading days: (1) Status quo/muddle through (40%) centered at $97 with crisis-era volatility, (2) Deal/de-escalation (20%) with Iran MOU approval pushing toward $88-92, (3) Escalation (15%) from fresh US-Iran clashes pushing toward $106-112, (4) Partial progress (15%) with mild decline toward $94-95, and (5) Major shock (10%) with extreme escalation toward $112-132.

The geopolitical backdrop is the 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis, with the strait blocked since February. Prices crashed from $126 peak (March) to $93 (late May) on ceasefire hopes, then bounced to $97 on renewed US-Iran clashes (June 3-4). Trump said June 1 that a deal was possible within a week, but simultaneous military exchanges (Iran attacking US bases in Bahrain/Kuwait, US striking Iran) maintain upside risk. TradingEconomics forecasts $92.30 by end of Q2. Realized daily volatility is ~3.5% (annualized ~55-60%), justifying wide tails. Distribution is slightly right-skewed due to asymmetric upside from supply-shock escalation scenarios.

factors.description: Current CBQ26 price ~$97 is the natural center. Status quo persistence with ongoing Iran-US ceasefire but no full deal = sideways trading around current level.
Trump said deal with Iran possible within a week (June 1). A deal reopening Hormuz would rapidly unwind risk premium, pushing prices toward $85-90. 60-day MOU already reached on May 28.
Fresh US-Iran military clashes continue (June 3-4): Iran attacked US bases in Bahrain/Kuwait, US struck Iranian military sites. Escalation risk is real and would spike prices toward $110-120+.
Downtrend from $117 peak (May 18) to $93 (May 29) reflects market pricing in de-escalation. Recent bounce to $97 may be a dead cat bounce within a larger downtrend as deal expectations build.
TradingEconomics forecasts $92.30 by end of Q2 2026, suggesting market consensus leans toward further decline from current $97.
Extremely high realized volatility (annualized ~55-60%) during this crisis period means wide tails are justified. Daily moves of 5-10% have occurred multiple times in recent weeks.

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary: ICE Brent Crude (CBQ26) on June 12, 2026

**Median: $96.86/bbl** | **80% CI: $86.81 – $112.64**

### Key Data Points
- **Current price**: CBQ26 at ~$97 (June 4, 2026)
- **Recent range**: $91–$117 over past 3 weeks
- **Crisis context**: Strait of Hormuz blocked since Feb 2026; ongoing US-Iran military clashes despite ceasefire

### Scenario Analysis (Monte Carlo, 500K simulations)
| Scenario | Weight | Median | P10–P90 |
|----------|--------|--------|---------|
| Status quo | 40% | $97 | $88–$107 |
| Deal/de-escalation | 20% | $90 | $83–$99 |
| Escalation | 15% | $106 | $94–$120 |
| Partial progress | 15% | $95 | $88–$102 |
| Major shock | 10% | $113 | $96–$132 |

### Key Drivers
- **Bearish**: Trump said deal within a week (June 1), 60-day MOU framework exists, TradingEconomics forecasts $92.30 by end of Q2
- **Bullish**: Fresh US-Iran clashes (attacks on bases, ships), Strait of Hormuz still blocked, crisis-era volatility ~55-60% annualized
- **Confidence**: Low (0.35) — genuine binary catalysts (deal vs. escalation) could move prices $10-15 in either direction within the 6-day window
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will the ICE Brent crude oil front-month futures settlement price be on June 12, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43773)
[Check out Brent Crude's stock price (@LCO.1) in real time](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/@LCO.1)
[Brent Crude Oil Last Day Financ (BZ=F) Stock Historical Prices & Data - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/BZ=F/history/)
[Crude Oil Prices: Brent - Europe (DCOILBRENTEU) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DCOILBRENTEU)
[Oil prices turn lower as U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension awaits Trump approval](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/28/oil-prices-us-strikes-in-iran-revive-strait-of-hormuz-turmoil-fears.html)
[Brent crude oil price on June 03, 2026 at 5:00 PM EDT?](https://kalshi.com/markets/kxbrentd/brent-oil-daily/kxbrentd-26jun0317)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the official settlement price, in US dollars per barrel, of the front-month ICE Brent Crude Futures contract for the trading session of Friday, June 12, 2026, as reported on Barchart's price history page for that contract: https://www.barchart.com/futures/quotes/CBQ26/price-history/historical (the August 2026 contract, symbol CBQ26, which is the front-month contract on that date). For example, a settlement of $87.45 per barrel resolves as 87.45. If Barchart is unavailable or does not display the settlement, the value reported by ICE's official Brent Crude Futures data page (https://www.ice.com/products/219/Brent-Crude-Futures/data) or by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) will be used.
fine_print: If June 12, 2026 is unexpectedly not a trading day for ICE Brent futures, the settlement for the most recent prior trading day in the week of June 8-12, 2026 will be used. If the August 2026 contract has unexpectedly expired or is no longer the front-month on June 12, 2026, the then-current front-month ICE Brent contract's settlement on that date will be used.

input.anchor: Current CBQ26 futures price: $97 (June 3-4 settlement). For a 6-trading-day horizon, the futures price itself is the best anchor/center for the distribution.
input.assessment:
This is a short-horizon commodity price forecast in an extremely volatile geopolitical environment. The Strait of Hormuz has been largely blocked since February 2026 due to US-Israel-Iran conflict, creating the largest oil supply disruption since the 1970s. Prices peaked at ~$126 in March, declined to ~$93 by late May on ceasefire hopes, and have bounced to ~$97.

Key tension: Trump says a deal is imminent (within a week as of June 1), which would be bearish for oil. But simultaneous military clashes (Iran attacking US bases, US striking Iran) suggest the situation remains highly volatile and could easily escalate. The 60-day MOU reached May 28 awaits Trump approval.

My distribution centers on ~$97 (current price) with:
- 25% weight on deal/de-escalation scenarios pulling prices to $88-92
- 40% weight on status quo / sideways trading around $95-100
- 25% weight on escalation scenarios pushing toward $106-120
- 10% weight on major shock / extreme escalation toward $112-132

The resulting distribution has P10=$86.8 and P90=$112.6, reflecting the genuine two-sided uncertainty. The mean ($98.6) is slightly above the median ($96.9) due to the asymmetric upside from escalation scenarios.
input.calibration_notes: The distribution width (P10-P90 = ~$26) is consistent with the crisis-era daily volatility of ~3.5%. Over 6 trading days, a random walk at this volatility produces σ ≈ $8.3, so 80% CI ≈ ±$10.7 from center, which gives roughly $86-108. My distribution is slightly wider due to the scenario mixing (especially escalation scenarios with higher vol), which is appropriate given the genuine geopolitical tail risk.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Will Trump approve the 60-day MOU with Iran? If yes, when? 2. Will the Strait of Hormuz actually reopen to normal traffic? Even with a deal, logistics take time. 3. Will fresh military clashes escalate into a broader conflict? 4. Could OPEC+ production changes offset or amplify the crisis impact?
input.tool_audit: Useful: CNBC quote for current CBQ26 price ($97.07/$97.81), FRED DCOILBRENTEU for daily spot price history, Wikipedia for Hormuz crisis context, TradingEconomics for current price and forecast. Empty/limited: Barchart and ICE pages didn't render well. Yahoo Finance returned wrong ticker (BZ=F mapped to KANZHUN). CNBC article on ceasefire MOU was very informative.
input.update_triggers: 1. Trump announces approval/rejection of Iran MOU (±$5-8 move). 2. Major military escalation (new Iranian missile attack, US strikes on Iran) (±$8-15 move). 3. Strait of Hormuz confirmed reopening to commercial traffic (+$10-15 decline). 4. OPEC+ emergency production decision (±$3-5 move).
input.process_reflection: The process worked reasonably well for this question. Web search + fetch provided the geopolitical context I needed. FRED data was excellent for historical price series. The Monte Carlo simulation was essential - this is exactly the kind of question where hand-picking percentiles would be unreliable. The scenario mixture approach captures the genuine bimodal risk (deal vs escalation). One friction point: Yahoo Finance's BZ=F returned KANZHUN stock data instead of Brent futures, which is a known symbol confusion. Would have been nice to have direct futures data API access.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Detailed Review

### Resolution Criteria
Resolution criteria are fully provided and the agent correctly engaged with them. The agent identified CBQ26 (August 2026 ICE Brent) as the target contract, Barchart as the primary resolution source, and correctly centered the forecast on the futures contract price rather than the Brent spot price. The fine print about alternative trading days and contract rollover was acknowledged.

### Factor-by-Factor Verification

**Factor 1** (Current CBQ26 ~$97, logit=+1.5): VERIFIED. CNBC @LCO.1 quote in trace shows prev close $97.81, intraday $97.07. TradingEconomics shows $97.01. These are direct tool results from fetch_url and web_search calls.

**Factor 2** (Trump deal within a week, 60-day MOU, logit=+1.0): VERIFIED. Vision Times article in trace: "Trump said on Monday, June 1 that negotiations between the United States and Iran are progressing smoothly, and the two sides may reach an agreement within the next week." CBS News confirms Trump "recently edited possible U.S.-Iran agreement." CNBC article confirms the 60-day MOU was reached on May 28 and was awaiting Trump approval.

**Factor 3** (Fresh US-Iran clashes June 3-4, logit=+1.2): VERIFIED. TradingEconomics snippet: "Following a US strike on an empty oil tanker bound for Iran on Tuesday, Iran launched attacks on US naval bases in Bahrain and Kuwait, as well as commercial vessels." Barchart headline: "Fresh Clashes Between the US and Iran Boost Crude Oil Prices." Both from direct web_search results.

**Factor 4** ($117 peak May 18 → $93 May 29 → $97, logit=+0.7): VERIFIED with minor rounding. FRED DCOILBRENTEU shows spot peak at $116.73 on May 18 (not $117, but close). FRED spot was $92.88 on May 29. CBQ26 settled at $91.12 on May 29 per Metaculus background. Bounce to ~$97 confirmed by CNBC data. The "dead cat bounce" interpretation is the agent's judgment, which is reasonable to include as a factor.

**Factor 5** (TradingEconomics forecast $92.30, logit=+0.5): VERIFIED. TradingEconomics fetch_url result: "Expected to trade at $92.30 USD/BBL by end of the quarter."

**Factor 6** (Annualized vol ~55-60%, logit=+0.8): VERIFIED. Agent's own code execution shows: "Annualized volatility: 60.8%" for the full period and "Annualized vol: 54.9%" for recent 8-day returns. Daily moves of 5-10% confirmed: the log returns include -9.92% (May 6) and +5.66% (June 1).

### Double-Counting Check
- Factors 1 and 4 overlap mildly: both reference the current price level, but Factor 4 adds directional momentum interpretation (downtrend). Not pure double-counting.
- Factors 2 and 4 have some shared evidence (de-escalation driving the decline), but Factor 2 is forward-looking (deal possibility) while Factor 4 is backward-looking (observed price trend).
- No severe double-counting detected.

### Wrong-Direction Check
All factor logit signs are consistent with descriptions. Factor 6 is about distribution width, not direction, and is appropriately handled.

### Contradictory Assessment Check
The narrative concludes with median ~$96.9, P10=$86.8, P90=$112.6. The Monte Carlo simulation produces median $96.86, P10=$86.81, P90=$112.64. The assessment is entirely consistent with the simulation output and factor analysis.

### Regime-Spanning Data Window
The agent computed volatility from FRED data spanning May 5 – June 1, which includes a documented regime break (stable_since: May 27, shift_magnitude: 12.86, direction: down). However, the agent explicitly acknowledged this and handled it appropriately: (1) noted the bootstrap had downward drift bias from the regime transition, (2) used zero drift in the base case scenario to correct, and (3) justified the high volatility as representative of the genuine crisis-era uncertainty. The scenario-based approach (deal vs. escalation vs. status quo) further mitigates regime-spanning contamination.

### Counterargument Quality
Substantive and well-constructed. Identifies a real alternative scenario (deal within Trump's one-week timeline → prices gap down $8-10), questions the 10% "Major Shock" weight, and acknowledges the downtrend momentum. References specific evidence (Trump timeline, $117→$93 precedent, both sides' willingness to negotiate). This is a genuine adversarial examination.

### Confidence Assessment
0.35 is appropriately low for a volatile commodity forecast in a geopolitical crisis with genuine binary catalysts (deal vs. escalation) within the 6-trading-day window.

### Probability Assessment
Center of $96.9 is anchored directly on the CBQ26 futures price of ~$97, which is the correct starting point for a 6-day horizon forecast. The P10-P90 range of $86.8-$112.6 (~26.8% width relative to center) appropriately reflects the extremely high realized volatility and genuine two-sided geopolitical uncertainty. The slight right skew (upside tail from escalation) is well-justified by the asymmetric nature of supply shock risks. My own estimate based on the evidence would be very similar: center ~$96-97, P10 ~$85-88, P90 ~$110-115.

### Worldview Consistency
Reviewed worldview entries for Brent crude (May 2026 entries at ~$109-114) and Iran war ceasefire negotiations. These are stale (from May 5) and reflect prices 1 month prior. No contradictions — the market has moved substantially since then (from ~$110 to ~$97) due to the de-escalation trajectory described in the agent's factors.

### Tool Audit
Agent claims "0 errors" — ground truth confirms 34 calls, 0 errors (0.0% error rate). Agent notes Barchart/ICE "didn't render well" and Yahoo Finance returned wrong ticker (KANZHUN) — both confirmed in the trace. These were not tool errors per se but produced unhelpful content. No discrepancy in tool audit claims.

### Summary
No hallucinated evidence, no significant double-counting, no wrong-direction factors, no contradictory assessment, resolution criteria properly engaged, well-calibrated probability assessment, substantive counterargument, and appropriate confidence level. The forecast is solid.
